title: Macrotask
body:
## Definition

A **macrotask** (task) is a queued callback from timers, I/O, UI events, or `setImmediate` (Node) — processed one at a time per [event loop](concept:event-loop) turn after [microtasks](concept:microtask) drain.

## Why it exists

Browser and Node schedule network, timers, and input as separate tasks so microtasks (Promises) can complete consistently first.

## How it works

`setTimeout`, `setInterval`, DOM event handlers, and message channel callbacks are macrotasks. One macrotask runs per loop iteration (then all microtasks again).

## Example

```js
setTimeout(() => console.log("timeout"), 0); // macrotask
setTimeout(() => console.log("timeout 2"), 0); // next loop iteration
```

## Common confusion

`setTimeout(fn, 0)` minimum delay is clamped (often ~4ms in nested timers). Background tabs throttle macrotasks heavily.

## In practice

Use macrotask timing for yielding to browser paint; use microtasks for Promise consistency. `requestIdleCallback` is yet another scheduling lane.

## Definition

A **[macrotask](concept:macrotask)** (task) is a queued callback from [timers](concept:timers), I/O, UI events, or [setImmediate](concept:timers) (Node) — processed one at a time per [event loop](concept:event-loop) turn after [microtasks](concept:microtask) drain.

## Why it exists

Browser and Node schedule network, [timers](concept:timers), and input as separate tasks so [microtasks](concept:microtask) ([Promises](concept:promise)) can complete consistently first.

## How it works

[setTimeout](concept:timers), [setInterval](concept:timers), [DOM](concept:dom) [event handlers](concept:typescript-react-events), and message channel callbacks are [macrotasks](concept:macrotask). One [macrotask](concept:macrotask) runs per loop [iteration](concept:iterator) (then all [microtasks](concept:microtask) again).

## Example

```js
setTimeout(() => console.log("timeout"), 0); // macrotask
setTimeout(() => console.log("timeout 2"), 0); // next loop iteration
```

## Common confusion

`setTimeout(fn, 0)` minimum delay is clamped (often ~4ms in nested [timers](concept:timers)). Background tabs [throttle](concept:debounce-throttle) [macrotasks](concept:macrotask) heavily.

## In practice

Use [macrotask](concept:macrotask) timing for yielding to browser paint; use [microtasks](concept:microtask) for [Promise](concept:promise) consistency. `requestIdleCallback` is yet another scheduling lane.
